title: M3 operational guards: artifact config-stamp pairing + strict --assets filter
source: 2026-07-03 M3 final whole-branch review (SDD execution of the M3 plan)
why: Two cross-task findings from the final review that protect research discipline rather than fix defects. (1) The long/short reporter pairs oof_predictions.parquet with events_side_fwd.parquet by index only; a barrier-param change re-run into the same output_dir (or a Phase-D error leaving a stale oof) would silently pair artifacts from different configurations — a config-hash stamp written into both artifacts and checked by the reporter closes it. (2) run_pooled_equity_d1 --assets silently drops unknown tickers; under B0003 caveat 2 a silently shrunken pool is an unrecorded trial — raise on subset - set(universe) instead.
value_rationale: Guards the frozen-universe/trial-count discipline that the M3 methodology depends on.
scope: Stamp a config hash (universe path + triple_barrier + date_range + primary params) into events_side_fwd.parquet metadata and the per-asset summary.json; reporter refuses mismatched pairs
run_pooled_equity_d1: raise ValueError when --assets contains tickers not in the universe segment

history.reason: logged from M3 final branch review
